sn44.9:1.1: Then Vacchagotta the wanderer went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn44.9:1.2: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
sn44.9:2.1: “Master Gotama, a few days ago a large number of contemplatives, brahmans, and wanderers of various sects were sitting together in the Debating Hall when this conversation arose among them:
sn44.9:2.2: ‘This Pūraṇa Kassapa—the leader of a community, the leader of a group, the teacher of a group, honored and famous, esteemed as holy by the mass of people—
sn44.9:2.3: describes a disciple who has died and passed on in terms of places of rebirth:
sn44.9:2.4: “That one is reborn there; that one is reborn there.”
sn44.9:2.5: Even when the disciple is an ultimate person, a foremost person, attained to the foremost attainment, Pūraṇa Kassapa describes him, when he has died and passed on, in terms of places of rebirth:
sn44.9:2.6: “That one is reborn there; that one is reborn there.”
sn44.9:3.5: This Ajita Kesakambala—the leader of a community, the leader of a group, the teacher of a group, honored and famous, esteemed as holy by the mass of people—
sn44.9:3.6: describes a disciple who has died and passed on in terms of places of rebirth:
sn44.9:3.7: “That one is reborn there; that one is reborn there.”
sn44.9:3.8: Even when the disciple is an ultimate person, a foremost person, attained to the foremost attainment, Ajita Kesakambala describes him, when he has died and passed on, in terms of places of rebirth:
sn44.9:3.9: “That one is reborn there; that one is reborn there.”
sn44.9:4.1: “This contemplative Gotama—the leader of a community, the leader of a group, the teacher of a group, honored and famous, esteemed as holy by the mass of people—
sn44.9:4.2: describes a disciple who has died and passed on in terms of places of rebirth:
sn44.9:4.3: “That one is reborn there; that one is reborn there.”
sn44.9:4.4: But when the disciple is an ultimate person, a foremost person, attained to the foremost attainment, the contemplative Gotama does not describe him, when he has died and passed on, in terms of places of rebirth:
sn44.9:4.5: “That one is reborn there; that one is reborn there.”
sn44.9:4.7: “He has cut through craving, severed the fetter, and by rightly breaking through conceit has made an end of suffering & stress.”’
sn44.9:4.8: “So I was simply befuddled. I was uncertain:
sn44.9:4.9: How is the teaching of Gotama the contemplative to be understood?”
sn44.9:5.1: “Of course you are befuddled, Vaccha. Of course you are uncertain.
sn44.9:5.2: When there is a reason for befuddlement in you, uncertainty arises.
sn44.9:5.3: I designate the rebirth of one who has sustenance, Vaccha, and not of one without sustenance.
sn44.9:5.4: Just as a fire burns with sustenance and not without sustenance,
sn44.9:5.5: even so I designate the rebirth of one who has sustenance and not of one without sustenance.”
sn44.9:6.1: “But, Master Gotama, at the moment a flame is being swept on by the wind and goes a far distance, what do you designate as its sustenance then?”
sn44.9:6.2: “Vaccha, when a flame is being swept on by the wind and goes a far distance, I designate it as wind-sustained,
sn44.9:6.3: for the wind is its sustenance at that time.”
sn44.9:6.4: “And at the moment when a being sets this body aside and is not yet reborn in another body, what do you designate as its sustenance then?”
sn44.9:6.5: “Vaccha, when a being sets this body aside and is not yet reborn in another body, I designate it as craving-sustained,
sn44.9:6.6: for craving is its sustenance at that time.”
